location.office_time_required: no required commute; occasional travel for off-sites

level_signals: We're looking for a senior to staff level product manager
6+ years experience in the software industry
leading the development of products end-to-end
a lot of ownership while remaining in the details day to day

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years experience in the software industry
Previous experience in engineering and/or design roles
Shameless curiosity, a propensity to question things deeply
Perform up-front discovery through user interviews, competitive research, data analysis, etc. and present findings to the team with conviction and rigor
leading the development of products end-to-end, from conception through development and rollout
This role is open to candidates based in North America or Europe time zones.

scope.surface: Product development system for teams and agents; AI-era software development tooling
core_responsibilities: Perform up-front discovery through user interviews, competitive research, data analysis, etc. and present findings to the team with conviction and rigor
Help engineers and designers deeply understand user needs, empowering them to make great product decisions
Craft messaging around upcoming features that resonates with users
Enable sales and marketing teams to effectively communicate with and win with customers

compensation.equity_form: equity with employee-friendly terms including early exercise in the US and extended exercise windows

green_flags: Remote-first with no required commute; North America and Europe eligible
Clear seniority signal: senior to staff level
Strong product culture with emphasis on craftsmanship and deep focus
Employee-friendly equity terms (early exercise, extended windows) explicitly called out
Generous benefits: 5 weeks PTO, 4 months parental leave, paid sabbatical after 4 years
Well-known, high-growth company (25,000+ customers including OpenAI, Coinbase, Ramp) with Series C funding
Encourages ownership and influence over product direction end-to-end
red_flags: No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization requirements — unclear for international candidates
Requirement for previous engineering and/or design experience is a hard filter that narrows the pool significantly
Benefits vary by country ('Health coverage based on country requirements') — could disadvantage some regions

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'senior to staff level' language in JD body)
scope.team_size_estimate (described as 'small, focused team' but exact number not stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'product-led company' and B2B customer list)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred as false from remote-first policy; not stated explicitly)
ambiguities: 'Previous experience in engineering and/or design roles' is listed as a bullet under 'What we're looking for' without 'required' language, but it appears in an unconditional list alongside other hard requirements — treated as hard/must-have here.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not mentioned anywhere in the JD — left as 'unclear'.
The JD says 'North America or Europe time zones' for location but does not specify individual cities or countries — left as remote-global with empty cities_listed.
